titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Ray DiPalma Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff and Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 7840525.
This collection contains 19 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 710 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday February 24, 2026 at 23:16
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Collections consists of correspondence, writings, printed material, artwork, photographs, audiovisual material, computer media, and personal papers by and relating to American poet and visual artist Ray DiPalma.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2005.
July 2006 acquisition: purchased from Ray DiPalma on the George B. Alvord Fund, 2006.
May 2018 acquisition: purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 39-40, 111 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 51, 110 (computer disks): Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Ray DiPalma Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Ray DiPalma Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and in some instances minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below according to month and year of acquisition.

The finding aid for this collection is compiled from individual preliminary lists for each acquisition that were created at or around the time of receipt by the library. The preliminary lists were migrated to comply with current archival descriptive standards and merged into a single file in 2007-2008. As part of the migration, modifications were made to the formatting of individual lists; however, the content of the lists was neither modified nor verified.

As a rule, descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
Former call numbers: Uncat MSS 796 and Uncat MSS 925.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Ray DiPalma (1943-)
bioghist.p: Ray DiPalma, American poet and visual artist, was born in New Kensington, Pennsylvania and educated at Duquesne University (1966) and the University of Iowa (1968). DiPalma has published over 30 books of poetry, translations, and graphic work, including House of Keys (2010), The Ancient Use of Stone: Journals and Daybooks 1998-2008 (2009), Pensieri (2009), and Further Apocrypha (2009). Earlier collections include Numbers and Tempers: Selected Early Poems, 1966-1986 (1993), Le Tombeau de Reverdy (1997), Provocations (1994), Hôtel des Ruines (1993), and Planh (1979). His visual work has been collected by galleries and museums, including the J. Paul Getty Museum, the Museum of Modern Art (MoMA), and the Art Institute of Chicago. He has received awards and fellowships from the National Endowment for the Arts, the New York Foundation for the Arts, and the Fund for Poetry. DiPalma lives in New York City and teaches at the School of Visual Arts.
Biographical information taken from The Poetry Foundation website at http://www.poetryfoundation.org/bio/ray-dipalma

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, printed material, artwork, photographs, audiovisual material, computer media, and personal papers by and relating to American poet and visual artist Ray DiPalma. It includes a large amount of correspondence with Vincent Dussol, DiPalma's French translator, and American poets Merrill Gilfillan, Ken McCullough, Ron Silliman, and Paul Vangelisti, as well as Mail Art. Other correspondents include authors and visual artists chiefly of DiPalma's generation, such as Bruce Andrews, Charles Bernstein, Guy Davenport, Lyn Hejinian, Peter Klappert, Michael Lally, and Tom Raworth. DiPalma's literary and artistic output includes drafts, proofs, and printed versions of written work and representations of graphic work dating from the mid 1960s. Supplementing this professional output are printed materials, including clippings and publicity, and sound recordings of readings. Also found in the collection are various personal papers, including diaries, notebooks, journals, and calendars.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: June 2005 Acquisition, July 2006 Acquisition, and May 2018 Acquisition.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 39 (reel-to-reel tapes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 40 (audiocassettes): Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 51 (computer disks): Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight groupings: Correspondence, Writings and Other Papers, Artists' Books, Graphic Works, Personal Papers, Printed Material, Miscellaneous Material, and Audiovisual Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The June 2005 Acquisition consists of correspondence, writings, printed material, artwork, photographs, audiovisual material, computer media, and personal papers.
Former call number: Uncat MSS 796.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2005.

scopecontent.p: Contains "Wet Loom Star"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Billy Collins, Noima Cole, Johanna Drucker, Harry Reese, Alan Davies, Patty Oldenberg, Robert Creeley, Arthur Vogelsang, Thomas D. Clark, and others

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The July 2006 Acquisition contains a small amount of printed material.
Former call number: Uncat MSS 925.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ray DiPalma on the George B. Alvord Fund, 2006.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 110 (digital media): Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 111 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six groupings: Correspondence, Writings and Other Papers, Graphic Works, Personal Papers, Photographs, and Computer and Audiovisual Media.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The May 2018 acquisition consists of correspondence, writings, artwork, personal papers, photographs, audiovisual material, digital files, and other papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence consists of letters and printed email exchanges with poets, writers, translators, editors, publishers, artists, family, friends, and others. Included are incoming and outgoing correspondence files for Vincent Dussol, DiPalma's French translator and close friend, and American poet Ken McCullough.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three sub-groupings: Vincent Dussol, Ken McCullough, and Incoming Correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily incoming and outgoing correspondence of DiPalma and Dussol. The files also contain correspondence of Merrill Gilfillan, Lyn Hejinian, Michael Lally, and Paul Vangelisti.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in chronological order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incoming and outgoing correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in chronological order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, followed by chronological correspondence files, which comprise the bulk of this sub-grouping.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings consist of notes, drafts, manuscripts, typescripts, proofs, and related materials for DiPalma's published and unpublished poems, books, notebooks, and other written works. The series includes files for DiPalma's collaborations with other writers and translations of DiPalma's work, as well as writings of others, some of which contain DiPalma's holograph annotations and edits, and accompanying correspondence. There are also printing templates for Hot Bird MFG , DiPalma's small press poetry magazine.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four sub-groupings: Writings, Collaborations, Writings of Others, and Hot Bird MFG .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

did.unittitle: The Ancient Use of Stone , galley proofs
The Ancient Use of Stone , page proofs, bound
"Black Maps," "Obloquium & Committee of Tidings," "Further Apocrypha," and "Hôtel des Ruines," typescript printouts
"By Way of Introduction," signed 1998, with holograph notes
"City in the City: The Beatitudes of Loss," initial drafts
Collages, poems, drafts, and fragments, April – June 2016
"The Factory Floor is Marble: Poems, Notes, and False Starts"
"From If to Why" with holograph notes
"Literature is about finding new places for evil"
Longhand notes, typed drafts, revisions, poems, and fragments, June – August 2005
Longhand notes, typed drafts, revisions, poems, and fragments, April – June 2006
"Obloquium," "Notes on the Disclosures of Form," "Breaking—and Entries," drafts, revisions and finished writings
"Purgavago," with holograph notes, drafts, notebooks, and correspondence with Paul Vangelisti
"Ray DiPalma's Anthology of Personally Favorite Blues and Rock Lyrics"

Stele broadside set of "Scelerous cursitors," "From The Ancient Use of Stone ," "Cargo," "What I want cut," "Promise the Perverse and Offer a Chinese Pillow," "Fragment," "Kafka's Golf Game," "I Savv the Kingg of Spann," "Microbook I/II/III"
"Two Sonnets," "Four Shelley Centos," "Six Byron Centos," and "Ten Days," typescripts
"Would, The Notional Field" holograph and typescript

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author.

did.unittitle: L'usage ancien de la pierre ( The Ancient Use of Stone ), translation with correspondence
Gilfillan, Merrill, and Darrell Gray, "Under the Dragon's Shadow"
Hocquard, Emmanuel, "Causes possibles et formes d'une relation entre deux poetes de part et d'autre de l'atlantique"
Vangelisti, Paul, "Uptown Vaunt," broadside with correspondence
Yarmolinsky, Ben, song settings for "Mansion Avenue," "Little Mirror," "Harp Fool Harp," "Accurate Beam," "Penny Paper," and "What is Sung"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author.

did.unittitle: DiPalma, Elizabeth, "Five Studies for Numbers and Tempers"
"Views of World Ending: Ayn Rand's Atlas Shrugged , Cormac McCarthy's The Road ," essay printouts, corrected
McCullough, Ken, Broken Gates , typescript printouts with correspondence
Raworth, Tom, "There are few people who put on any clothes (Starring It)"
Schaefer, Standard and Paul Vangelisti, "How to Succeed with Women"
"The Arc of the Day," typescript printouts
Suzor, Sarah, "Of the Head," typescript printouts with correspondence
"A Capable Hand, or Maps for a Lost Dog," draft
"The Lyrical Obsession with Matter: Marinetti's 'Technical Manifesto of Futurist Literature' and West Coast Poetry," typescript printouts, corrected, with correspondence
"Maps for a Lost Dog," typescript printouts, corrected
Unsorted work by others with holograph annotations

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in chronological order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Graphic Works consist of DiPalma's artwork, including visual poetry, graphics, collages, and rubber stamp works, both as works in progress and finished pieces. The series also contains many of the materials and tools used to create his artwork.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three sub-groupings: General, Collected Materials for Collages, and Rubber Stamps.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in chronological order, with undated items filed alphabetically at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Untitled collage and rubber stamp work
Binder of 23 rubber stamp artworks, 8 collages, and 3 hand-colored photocopies
Binder of small collage works and "Towards a Personal Alphabet: A Work in Progress" photocopy
"The People Out on the Lagoon," with collage materials
"The Historical Scrapbook," collage with handmade paper, book parts, baggage labels
"Plutarch's Lives," collage with handmade paper, book parts, baggage labels
"Thus Spake Zarathustra," collage with handmade paper, book parts, baggage labels

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping contains materials incorporated in DiPalma's collage art, such as reproductions of his own printed or stamped image works, and a variety of ephemera he collected. Ephemera encompasses a wide range of material types and formats, including clippings, postcards, stereograph cards, commercial packaging boxes, tickets, brochures, and other printed material, as well as photographs and textiles. Some materials date from the 1800s and early 1900s.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Image bank of stock images by DiPalma
"Peter Coddle's trip to New York" game by Selchow & Righter Co.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains rubber stamps DiPalma used to create visual poetry, graphic works, and collages. The majority appear to be ready made. There are seventeen boxes of stamps, including sets in their original packaging or stored in various containers by DiPalma, as well as sixteen loose stamps. Types of stamps include handstamps, wood handle stamps, hand carved woodblock printing stamps, office and desk stamps, rubber type set, and decorative stamp kits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal Papers consist of ephemera related to DiPalma's public appearances and performances, professional files, datebooks, notebooks, and calendars, miscellaneous personal documents, and other assorted papers. The series also contains artwork by other artists and poets, including friends of DiPalma.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: "Un palmier prend la mer," drafts of essay on DiPalma, author unknown
Ephemera [Acidic Ghost Spectral Reading Series poster]
Scrapbook of Duquesne University and University of Iowa
Small objects and "Voice" sign, with label "Doones Press"
Drawing by Joe Brainard for the cover of Doones no. 2
Drawing on map by Stephen Shrader for Doones no. 4, signed
Pen drawing of DiPalma by Stephen Shrader, signed and dated "Paris 3/71"
Ink drawings by Fielding Dawson, signed
Collage for Ray DiPalma, signed by unknown artist with note, "Thanks to Ed Plunkett, who sent a large packet this week."
Collage by Celine [Faure] Amundsen, with correspondence
Ink drawing by Alex Kneley, signed "for and after Ray DiPalma," with correspondence
Collage on paper bag, artist unknown
Two drawings of DiPalma by Elizabeth DiPalma

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping contains personal and professional photographs, including portraits of DiPalma, and photographs of family and friends, as well as color slides of DiPalma and his artwork.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by format.

did.unittitle: Mounted photographs, signed by E. Brandfass
Color slides, "Ray - Drawings/collages" and work by other artists

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two sub-groupings: Computer media, and Audiovisual media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 110 (digital media): Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be
requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 111 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested.
Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, born digital manuscripts, digital photos, and other materials on flash drives and optical media.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes audio and videocassette recordings of DiPalma's poetry readings and performances.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by format, and alphabetically by title.
